title: TOPIK I Authentic Mock Exam 02 — Guided Course

courses.title: Answering Simple Questions
objectives: Answer yes/no and wh-questions by echoing the question's pattern
Reply to congratulations and meal invitations with the right set phrase

new_vocabulary.en: to not exist, to not have
to get up, to wake up

new_grammar.explanation: Polite question asking whether someone or something is N; answer by repeating the noun with 네 or denying it with 아니요, N이/가 아니에요.
Asks whether something exists in a place; the natural negative answer replaces 있어요 with 없어요.
Speaker's promise or willingness, directed at the listener; very common when accepting gifts, favors, or tasks.

courses.review: You can now answer identity, existence, time, and person questions and reply naturally to gifts and meals.

courses.title: Where Am I? Places and Topics
objectives: Identify a place from the errand words you hear (post office, bakery, salon, pharmacy)
Find the topic that covers what both speakers are talking about

new_grammar.explanation: Expresses the speaker's desire to do something; common when stating an errand at a counter.
Joins two nouns (and) when ordering or listing items.
new_grammar.example: 크림빵 두 개하고 식빵 한 개 주세요.

new_grammar.explanation: Attaches to a time noun to mean 'every N'; 토요일마다 = every Saturday.

courses.review: You can now pin down a place from its errand words and name the topic that ties a short dialogue together.

courses.title: Reading Foundations: Topics and Particles
objectives: Choose the category word that both reading sentences are about
Pick the right particle for place of action (에서) and contrast (은/는)

new_grammar.explanation: Marks the place where an action is performed.
Beyond marking topics, 은/는 contrasts one item against another already mentioned.
new_grammar.example: 저는 우유를 마십니다. 동생은 안 마십니다.

new_grammar.explanation: Verbs ending in ㄹ drop the ㄹ before -ㅂ니다: 만들다 → 만듭니다, 살다 → 삽니다.

courses.review: You can now match a short passage to its topic and choose particles for location and contrast.

courses.title: Shopping, Gifts, and Notices
objectives: Track who is buyer and who is seller in a shopping scene
Read a notice or poster and spot the statement that does not match it

new_vocabulary.en: to use, to take advantage of

new_grammar.explanation: Polite request for the listener to do something for the speaker's benefit.
Grants permission; with 안 before the verb it means 'you may skip doing it'.
Marks the start and end of a time range.
new_grammar.example: 일곱 시부터 여덟 시까지 반값에 팝니다.

courses.review: You can now follow a shopping exchange and check a notice line by line for the false statement.

courses.title: Weekend Plans and Family Events
objectives: Map each activity to the right day or time slot while listening
Track who does what when two people split tasks for an event

new_grammar.explanation: Suggests an action to do together and asks the listener's opinion.
Expresses necessity or obligation: must/have to do something.
States the purpose of going somewhere.

courses.review: You can now keep two-day plans straight and assign each task to the right person in a planning dialogue.

courses.title: Opinions and Main Ideas
objectives: Find the claim a speaker's reasons are supporting, not the reasons themselves
Read a 'A-지만 B' sentence and take B as the writer's real opinion

new_vocabulary.en: to be good for the body/health
to jot down, to make a note

new_grammar.explanation: Two actions done at the same time by the same subject: eat while talking.
Indicates a result that happens to you rather than by choice: 사게 돼요 = you end up buying.
new_grammar.example: 메모를 안 하면 필요 없는 것까지 사게 돼요.

new_grammar.explanation: Concedes one point before stating the opposing, weightier point.

courses.review: You can now separate reasons from the main point and read concession sentences for the speaker's true stance.

courses.title: Details, Messages, and Reported Speech
objectives: Check companion, quantity, and time slots against a passage to catch flipped facts
Read a short note and state why the writer wrote it

new_grammar.explanation: Indirect quotation reporting what someone said.
Noun phrase meaning 'how to V'; often paired with 배우다/가르치다.
new_grammar.example: 어머니가 만두 만드는 법을 가르쳐 주셨습니다.

new_grammar.explanation: Polite way to ask whether someone can do a favor.
new_grammar.example: 꽃에 물을 좀 줄 수 있어요?

courses.review: You can now verify details against the text, read reported speech, and identify the purpose of a short message.

courses.title: Cooking and Home Stories
objectives: Order scrambled sentences using cause words and time clues
Pick the verb or ending a passage needs, including ability and trying patterns

new_grammar.explanation: Expresses a decision made (often with others) to do something.
To try doing something, often repeatedly while learning.
Ability/possibility; the verb before 수 must take -(으)ㄹ.

courses.review: You can now sequence a short narrative and choose the verb forms—ability, trying, and decisions—that cooking and home passages require.
